question_text: IFR and VFR flights are permitted, all flights are provided with air traffic control service and IFR flights are separated from other IFR flights and from VFR flights. VFR flights are separated from IFR flights and receive traffic information in respect of other VFR flights. Name the classification of airspace. from question bank
explanation: Section 3.1: Class C airspace permits both IFR and VFR; all flights receive ATC service, IFR separated from IFR and VFR, VFR separated from IFR and receives traffic info on other VFR.

question_text: What is the minimum recommended altitude for aircraft enroute fuel dumping? from question bank
explanation: Q-3.2 answer key: minimum recommended altitude for fuel dumping is 6000 ft.

question_text: In relation to the three entry sectors, the entry into the holding pattern shall be in accordance with its heading. from question bank
explanation: Q-3.3 states that entry sectors depend on heading, so the statement is true.

question_text: When two descending aircraft maintain the same IAS, and leading aircraft is at the lower level, the TAS of the leading aircraft will be: from question bank
explanation: At lower altitude, air density is higher, so for the same IAS, TAS is lower. Hence the leading aircraft at lower level has lower TAS.

question_text: At FL250 & above speed adjustment of Mach 0.01 will be equal to: from question bank
explanation: At FL250 and above, 0.01 Mach ≈ 6 knots IAS as per standard conversion.

question_text: Before authorizing an aircraft to dump fuel enroute, the controller must consider: from question bank

options.A: Minimum recommended altitude for fuel dumping.
options.B: Amount of fuel to be dumped.
options.D: Both a) and c) are correct.

explanation: The controller must consider minimum altitude and affected airspace; amount of fuel is not a primary consideration for the controller.

question_text: A containment value expressed as a distance in nautical miles from the intended position within which flights would be for at least 95% of the total flying time, is known as: from question bank
explanation: RNP type defines the containment value (e.g., RNP 4 means 4 NM) for 95% of the flight time.

question_text: Which of the following phases of emergency will start if the fuel of an aircraft in flight is considered to be insufficient to enable the aircraft to reach safely? from question bank
explanation: Insufficient fuel to reach safety constitutes grave and imminent danger, triggering the Distress Phase (DETRESFA).

question_text: The Aerodrome Reference Code is intended to determine: from question bank

options.D: Facilities suitable for the aeroplane expected to operate.

explanation: The Aerodrome Reference Code (Element 1 and 2) indicates the characteristics of the aeroplane (field length, wingspan) for which the aerodrome is suitable.

question_text: An aircraft cruising technique, resulting in a net increase in altitude as the aircraft mass decreases is called: from question bank
explanation: Definition of cruise climb: a technique where altitude increases as fuel burns and mass decreases, maintaining optimum performance.

question_text: Clearance limit shall be the destination aerodrome when there is reasonable assurance that prior coordination can be affected with the units under whose control the aircraft will subsequently come. from question bank
explanation: Q-3.11 states it is true; clearance limit can be destination if coordination is assured.

question_text: Which of the following factors must be considered before offering direct route to an aircraft? from question bank

options.D: All of the above answers are correct.

explanation: All three factors are important when considering direct routing.

question_text: Which of the following shall always be read back upon receipt by the flight crew? from question bank

options.A: Instruction to hold short of runway.

explanation: All of these are safety-critical and require readback as per ICAO and MATS.

question_text: If the level of an aircraft is reported in relation to QNH or QFE, the figures shall be followed by the word: from question bank

options.C: feet or meters whichever is applicable
options.D: None of the above answers is correct.

explanation: Phraseology requires QNH figures to be followed by 'altitude' and QFE by 'height', not just 'feet/meters'.

question_text: Clearances or instructions from ATC shall be read back by the flight crew. from question bank
explanation: Standard requirement: all ATC clearances and instructions must be read back.

question_text: It is not the responsibility of ATC to listen the read back from the flight crew. from question bank
explanation: ATC must listen and ensure readback is correct; it is their responsibility.

question_text: CPDLC is the abbreviation of: from question bank
explanation: Correct expansion: Controller-Pilot Data Link Communications (CPDLC) - note the plural 'Communications'.

question_text: Aircraft may be instructed to adjust speed in order to facilitate safe and orderly flow of air traffic by ATC. What considerations are needed before implementing speed control? from question bank

options.A: Speed control shall be subject to the conditions specified by appropriate authority.
options.B: Flight crew should be given adequate notice of planned speed control.
options.C: Application of speed control over a long period of time may affect aircraft fuel reserves.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

explanation: All three are important considerations before applying speed control.

question_text: Speed control shall not be applied to: from question bank

options.B: Aircraft entering or established in a holding pattern.
options.C: Aircraft flying inbound to destination aerodrome.
options.D: Both a) and b) are correct.

explanation: Speed control is not applied to aircraft in holding patterns; entering control zone is not prohibited.

question_text: Frequent speed adjustments including alternate speed increases or decreases should be avoided. from question bank
explanation: Frequent changes are undesirable; they increase pilot workload and fuel consumption.

question_text: What shall be the action by the ATC when flight crew indicates its inability to comply with the speed instructions? from question bank

options.B: ATC shall apply an alternative method to achieve desired spacing.
options.C: ATC shall inform operator about it.
options.D: ATC shall make a log entry.

explanation: ATC must find an alternative spacing method; reporting or logging is secondary.

question_text: Speed adjustments should be in multiples of 0.01 Mach At or above: from question bank
explanation: At or above FL250, speed adjustments are in 0.01 Mach increments.

question_text: Speed adjustments should be in multiples of 10 knots at levels below: from question bank
explanation: Below FL250, speed adjustments are in multiples of 10 knots.

question_text: Speed adjustments from ATC, in order to facilitate a safe and orderly flow of air traffic should be based on: from question bank
explanation: ATC speed instructions are normally given in IAS (indicated air speed).

question_text: In ATS Management ADS is an abbreviation of: from question bank
explanation: ADS stands for Automatic Dependent Surveillance.

question_text: Aircraft shall be advised when a speed control restriction is no longer required. from question bank
explanation: Pilots must be informed when speed restrictions are cancelled.

question_text: An aircraft operating on the maneuvering area is considered as part of: from question bank
explanation: By definition, 'air traffic' includes aircraft on the maneuvering area.

question_text: The difference between IAS and TAS is negligible for speed control below: from question bank
explanation: Below FL80, the difference between IAS and TAS is considered negligible.

question_text: When two aircraft maintaining same IAS are descending and leading aircraft is at lower level, the distance between the two aircraft will decrease unless a speed differential is applied. What is the desired speed differential to be applied: from question bank
explanation: A rule of thumb: 6 knots IAS per 1000 ft difference to maintain spacing.

question_text: An aircraft may be authorized to reduce speed, when a terminal delay has been notified to the aircraft. from question bank
explanation: If a delay is expected, reducing speed may be appropriate.

question_text: When applying speed control, an aircraft may be instructed by ATC to maintain: from question bank
explanation: ATC may instruct to maintain maximum, minimum, or minimum clean speed as needed.

question_text: Minimum clean speed specifies the minimum speed of the aircraft when no lift augmentation devices have been applied. from question bank
explanation: Clean configuration means no flaps, slats, etc., so minimum clean speed is the stall speed in clean config.

question_text: An aircraft may be asked to reduce speed and maintain high rate of descent. from question bank
explanation: Reducing speed while maintaining a high rate of descent can be difficult and is not normally instructed.

question_text: Only minor speed reductions of not more than 20 kts IAS should be used for aircraft on intermediate and final approach. from question bank
explanation: Large speed reductions on final are undesirable; limit to 20 kts.

question_text: Speed control should not be applied to the aircraft on final approach after passing: from question bank
explanation: After passing 4 NM on final, speed control is not applied.

question_text: Speed control shall not be applied to: from question bank

options.A: Aircraft of LIGHT wake turbulence category.
options.B: Aircraft with medium wake turbulence category.
options.C: Aircraft entering or established in a holding pattern.
options.D: None of the above is correct.

explanation: Speed control is not applied to aircraft in holding patterns; wake turbulence category is not a reason.

question_text: Instructions involving frequent changes of climb / descent rate should be avoided. from question bank
explanation: Frequent changes increase pilot workload and are to be avoided.

question_text: What shall be the action by the ATC when flight crew informs of their inability to comply with the specified rate of descent/climb instructions. from question bank

options.A: ATC shall file a report against the flight crew.
options.B: ATC shall file a report against the flight crew and apply an alternative method to achieve separation.
options.C: ATC shall apply an alternative method to achieve appropriate separation.
options.D: ATC shall inform appropriate ATS authorities.

explanation: ATC must find an alternative separation method; reporting is secondary.

question_text: Aircraft may not be advised when a rate of climb or descent restriction is no longer is required. from question bank
explanation: Must be advised when restriction is no longer required; the question has a typo ('is no longer is required') but answer is false.

question_text: Aircraft may be instructed to: from question bank

options.D: All of the above are correct.

explanation: ATC may instruct expedite climb, descent, or through a level.

question_text: A climbing aircraft may be instructed to: from question bank

options.A: Maintain a specified rate of climb.
options.B: Maintain a rate of climb equal to or greater than a specified value.
options.C: Maintain a rate of climb equal to or less than a specified value.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

explanation: All three types of rate instructions are possible.

question_text: A descending aircraft may be instructed to: from question bank

options.A: Maintain a specified rate of descent.
options.B: Maintain a rate of descent equal to or greater than a specified value.
options.C: Maintain a rate of descent equal to or less than a specified value.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

explanation: Same as for climb; all rate instructions are possible.

question_text: The controller should ascertain to which level climbing or descending aircraft can sustain a specified rate of climb or descent. from question bank
explanation: ATC must know aircraft performance to give achievable rates.

question_text: Controllers need not to be aware of aircraft performance. from question bank
explanation: Controllers must be aware of aircraft performance to give safe and achievable instructions.

question_text: When a change from IFR flight to VFR flight shall be acceptable? from question bank

options.A: When the message "CANCELLING MY IFR FLGHT" is initiated by the pilot.
options.B: When the aircraft has accepted and acknowledged the change from IFR to VFR flight.
options.C: When ATC has reasonable assurance that VFR conditions will prevail till the landing of the flight concerned at the destination.
options.D: None of the above is correct.

explanation: The pilot initiates cancellation of IFR flight; ATC acknowledges but does not need to assure conditions for the whole flight.

question_text: What should normally be the reply from ATC when a message containing specific information regarding the cancellation of IFR flight is received from the pilot. from question bank

options.A: IFR conditions likely to be encountered, should be advised if practicable.
options.B: ATC shall check the forecast with the met office.
options.C: Normally no other reply than the acknowledgement "IFR FLIGHT CANCELLED AT....... (Time)".
options.D: Both a) and c) are correct.

explanation: Standard reply is acknowledgement; if adverse conditions are likely, advice may be given.

question_text: What shall be the action by the ATC, when a message containing specific information regarding the change from IFR flight to VFR flight is received from the pilot? from question bank

options.A: ATC may not provide standard separation.
options.B: ATC shall inform other aircraft in the vicinity.
options.C: ATC shall advise all other ATS units to whom the flight plan was addressed.
options.D: None of the above is correct.

explanation: ATC must advise all ATS units that received the flight plan of the change.

question_text: Which of the following statement is correct about "Wake Turbulence"? from question bank

options.A: It describes the effects of air masses generated by the aircraft engines behind the aircraft.
options.B: It describes the effects of the rotating air masses generated behind the wing tips of large jet aircraft.
options.C: It describes the effects of the air masses ahead of the aircraft while landing or taking off.
options.D: None of the above is correct.

explanation: Wake turbulence is caused by wingtip vortices, not engine exhaust.

question_text: What will be the wake turbulence category of an aircraft with certified mass of 135999 kg. from question bank
explanation: Wake category: Heavy = 136,000 kg or more; Medium = 7,000 to 136,000 kg; Light = less than 7,000 kg. So 135,999 kg is Medium.

question_text: What will be the wake turbulence category of an aircraft if its certified mass is 7000 kg. from question bank
explanation: 7000 kg is exactly the threshold for Medium (7,000 kg and above but less than 136,000 kg), so Medium.

question_text: Which type of aircraft shall be kept well clear when helicopters are taxiing or hovering? from question bank
explanation: Light aircraft are most susceptible to rotor downwash and should be kept clear.

question_text: An aircraft in heavy wake turbulence category shall include the word "HEAVY" with its call sign: from question bank

options.A: In each call with ATS units
options.E: Only on initial contact and on final

explanation: The word HEAVY is used in the initial call sign contact, not every call.

question_text: In which item of the flight plan wake turbulence categories are specified? from question bank
explanation: Wake turbulence category is in Item 9 of the ICAO flight plan (aircraft type and wake category).

question_text: When an aircraft is descending and passing through the transition layer, the vertical position of the aircraft shall be expressed in terms of: from question bank
explanation: During descent through the transition layer, altitude is used (QNH), not flight level.

question_text: When an aircraft is climbing and passing through the transition layer, the vertical position of the aircraft shall be expressed in terms of: from question bank
explanation: During climb through transition layer, flight level is used (1013.2 hPa).

question_text: When an aircraft is at or below the transition altitude, the vertical position of the aircraft shall be expressed in terms of: from question bank
explanation: At or below transition altitude, altitude (QNH) is used.

question_text: When an aircraft is above the transition layer, the vertical position of the air shall be expressed in terms of: from question bank
explanation: Above transition layer, flight level is used.

question_text: If an aircraft is flying below the lowest usable flight level for an ATS route the vertical position of the flight will be expressed in terms of: from question bank
explanation: Below the lowest usable flight level, altitude is used.

question_text: Transition level can be defined as: from question bank

options.A: The lowest level available above the transition altitude.
options.B: The highest level available above the transition altitude.
options.C: The lowest level available below the transition altitude.
options.D: The highest level available below the transition altitude.
options.E: The level just above transition altitude

explanation: Transition level is the lowest flight level above transition altitude.

question_text: Two same speed aircraft at same level can be handed over to Karachi ACC by Lahore ACC keeping 20 NM longitudinal separation based on DME. from question bank
explanation: Longitudinal separation based on DME requires same DME station and specific minima; 20 NM may not be sufficient without speed differential.

question_text: One aircraft on track 215 degrees while the other is on track 155 degrees will be considered as: from question bank
explanation: 215° and 155° differ by 60°, which is less than 135° and not reciprocal, so crossing tracks.

question_text: One aircraft is on track 315 degrees while the other is on track 155 degrees they will be considered on: from question bank
explanation: 315° and 155° differ by 160°, which is between 135° and 225°, so reciprocal tracks.

question_text: One aircraft is on track 015 degrees while the other is on track 330 degrees. They will be considered as on: from question bank
explanation: 015° and 330° differ by 45°, which is less than 135° and not reciprocal, so crossing tracks.

question_text: Transition level shall be established by: from question bank
explanation: Any appropriate ATS unit can establish transition level, typically the ACC or APP.

question_text: Quetta Runway is equipped with ILS CAT I. from question bank
explanation: As per AIP, Quetta has ILS CAT I.

question_text: One of the objectives of Air Traffic Control Service is to expedite traffic. The basic purpose of this objective is to save fuel. from question bank
explanation: Expediting traffic helps reduce fuel burn.

question_text: Total number of airports in Pakistan as per AIP, from question bank
explanation: As per AIP Pakistan, there are 32 airports.

question_text: ATS routes outside CTA are class G below: from question bank
explanation: Outside CTA, ATS routes are class G below FL155.

question_text: The funnel area will start from the end of the runway. from question bank
explanation: Funnel area starts at 900 ft from the end of the runway.

question_text: Common transition level can be established for two or more aerodromes closely located provided: from question bank

options.A: A common transition altitude has been established for use.
options.C: A common circuit is being used.
options.D: Both a) and b) are correct.

explanation: Common transition level requires common transition altitude and coordinated procedures.

question_text: Cruising levels below the minimum flight altitude established by the state cannot be assigned unless: from question bank

options.A: Specifically authorized by the appropriate authority.
options.D: There is a bad weather en-route.

explanation: Only specific authorization allows assignment below minimum flight altitude.

question_text: To determine the lowest usable flight level for the whole or part of control area is the responsibility of: from question bank
explanation: ATS units determine the lowest usable flight level.

question_text: The lowest usable flight is that flight level which corresponds to, or is immediately above, the established minimum flight altitude: from question bank
explanation: Definition matches the statement.

question_text: The funnel area shall spread out to maximum width of either side of runway axis at a distance of 12100 ft. from question bank
explanation: At 12100 ft from runway end, the funnel width is 2000 ft each side.

question_text: Flight crew shall be provided with transitional level in due time prior to reaching it during descent. from question bank
explanation: Required to be given in advance.

question_text: Contours are the lines joining the points of equal pressure. from question bank
explanation: Contours join points of equal elevation, not pressure. Isobars join equal pressure. (Correction from review.)

question_text: Except when it is known that aircraft has already received the QNH altimeter setting, it should be included in: from question bank

options.D: All of the above are correct.

explanation: QNH must be included in descent clearance, taxi clearance, and circuit clearance if not already given.

question_text: Altimeter setting provided to aircraft shall be rounded up to the nearest upper whole hectopascal. from question bank
explanation: Altimeter settings are rounded to the nearest whole hectopascal (not necessarily upper).

question_text: Lines joining the places of equal atmospheric pressure are called Isogonals. from question bank
explanation: Correction: Isogonals are lines of equal magnetic variation; isobars are equal pressure.

question_text: When an aircraft is assigned a speed to maintain by ATC, the flight crew shall include this speed in: from question bank
explanation: Assigned speed must be included in position reports.

question_text: The length of clearway should not exceed half the length of the associated runway. from question bank
explanation: Clearway length is limited to half the runway length.

question_text: ADS reports shall be composed of data blocks. Which of the following is not a data block of an ADS report? from question bank
explanation: SSR is not a data block in ADS reports; ADS data blocks include Basic ADS, Ground vector, and Short term intent.

question_text: A runway turn pad is subjected to slow moving traffic making hard turns and consequently ______ on pavement. from question bank
explanation: Hard turns impose higher stresses on pavement.

question_text: AIP should be published from question bank
explanation: AIP is published in loose-leaf form for easy amendment.

question_text: An AIP shall have from question bank
explanation: AIP has three parts: GEN, ENR, AD.

question_text: The required minimum track separation between two departing aircraft using the same NDB at a distance of 15 NM from NDB is: from question bank
explanation: Minimum track separation at NDB is 30° for that distance.

question_text: The minimum possible separation based on distance for the aircraft on crossing tracks when the preceding aircraft is maintaining TAS 20 Kt or more faster: from question bank
explanation: For crossing tracks, 10 NM minimum when preceding is 20 kt faster.

question_text: For investigation purpose flight progress strips shall be retained at least for a period of: from question bank
explanation: Flight progress strips retained for 30 days.

question_text: In which chapter of Doc 4444/ ATM 501, the Separation Methods And Minima are specified: from question bank
explanation: Chapter 5 of Doc 4444 covers separation methods and minima.

question_text: Separation shall be provided between all flights in: from question bank

options.D: In class A and B airspace.

explanation: In Class A and B, separation is provided between all flights. In Class C, VFR are not separated from VFR.

question_text: Separation shall be provided between IFR flights in airspace of: from question bank

options.C: Class A, B, C and D.
options.D: Class A, B, C,D and E.

explanation: IFR separated from IFR in Classes A, B, C, D, and E.

question_text: Separation shall be provided between IFR and VFR flights in airspace of: from question bank

options.C: Class A, B, C and D.

explanation: IFR and VFR separated in Class B and C (and also D? Actually D separates IFR from IFR, and IFR from VFR? Let's check: In Class D, IFR separated from IFR and receive traffic info on VFR, but IFR and VFR are not necessarily separated? Actually ICAO: Class D – IFR and VFR flights are permitted, IFR flights are separated from other IFR flights and receive traffic information about VFR flights, VFR receive traffic info about all flights. So separation between IFR and VFR is not provided. So only Class B and C provide separation between IFR and VFR. So answer D is correct.

question_text: Separation shall be provided between IFR and Special VFR flight: from question bank

options.C: In class B and C airspace.
options.D: Irrespective of the class of airspace.

explanation: Special VFR flights are separated from IFR regardless of airspace class.

question_text: Separation shall be provided between VFR flights in: from question bank

options.D: Class A, B, C and D.

explanation: VFR flights are separated from other VFR only in Class B.

question_text: Under what circumstances, larger separation than the specified minima should be applied: from question bank

options.C: In case of technical problems with the flight

explanation: Larger separation may be needed for unlawful interference or navigational difficulties; technical problems may also warrant but not explicitly listed.

question_text: When one type of separation has failed, action must be taken to establish another type of separation. from question bank
explanation: If separation fails, a new type must be applied.

question_text: ATC shall normally authorize only one level for an aircraft beyond its control area. from question bank
explanation: Only one level is authorized beyond control area.

question_text: When cruise climb has been permitted by appropriate ATS authority, an aircraft shall be authorized to employ cruise climb: from question bank
explanation: Cruise climb is authorized between two levels.

question_text: An aircraft may be cleared to change its level: from question bank
explanation: Level change can be specified by time, rate, or place.

question_text: Cruising levels to the aircraft flying to the same destination shall be assigned in a manner that will be: from question bank

options.A: Correct for an approach sequence at the destination.
options.C: In accordance with the procedures at the destination aerodrome.

explanation: Assigned cruising levels should facilitate approach sequence at destination.

question_text: An aircraft at a cruising level shall normally have priority over other aircraft requesting that level. from question bank
explanation: Aircraft already at a level have priority over others requesting it.

question_text: When two or more aircraft are at the same level, the preceding aircraft shall normally have the priority. from question bank
explanation: Preceding aircraft has priority at same level.

question_text: An aircraft may be cleared to a level previously occupied by another aircraft after the latter has reported vacating it, except when: from question bank

options.B: The higher aircraft is effecting cruise climb.
options.C: There is difference in performance of the aircraft involved.

explanation: All three situations may prevent immediate clearance to a vacated level.

question_text: How two or more aircraft with different performance, established in the same holding pattern, can be given descent. from question bank

options.B: Both the aircraft must have each other insight.
options.D: Both a) and c) are correct.

explanation: Step descent and restricted rate are used; visual sighting is not required.

question_text: What does the abbreviation TCAS stands for? from question bank

options.B: Traffic Alert and collision avoidance system.

explanation: TCAS stands for Traffic Alert and Collision Avoidance System.

question_text: What will you understand when an aircraft is said to be established on track from the NDB: from question bank

options.A: Aircraft is moving away from the NDB.
options.B: Aircraft is moving towards the NDB.
options.C: Aircraft is established in NDB holding pattern.
options.D: Aircraft is passing abeam an NDB station.

explanation: Established 'on track from' means outbound from the NDB.

question_text: The larger separation between aircraft shall be provided than the specified minima when there is: from question bank
explanation: Wake turbulence requires larger separation; other conditions may not necessarily increase separation minima.

question_text: The airspace around a fuel dumping aircraft is affected by the fuel release is known as the Vapor Zone and other known traffic shall be separated from the aircraft dumping fuel by: from question bank

options.B: 15 minutes flying time or 50 NM behind 1000 ft above and 3000 ft below.
options.D: Both a) and b) are correct.

explanation: Separation for fuel dumping includes horizontal 10 NM not behind, or 15 min/50 NM behind with vertical buffers.

question_text: What will you understand when an ATS route is designated as RNP 10? from question bank

options.A: Navigation accuracy of plus or minus10 NM on a 95 percent containment basis is required.
options.B: It is 10th version of a RNAV route.
options.C: An aircraft is authorized to alter its headings but shall remain with 10 NM from the center line of the route.

explanation: RNP 10 means 95% containment within 10 NM.

question_text: Longitudinal separation may be established by requiring aircraft to: from question bank

options.B: Arrive over a geographical location at a specified time.
options.C: Hold over a geographical location until a specified time.

explanation: All these methods can establish longitudinal separation.

question_text: The tracks will be considered as same tracks if the angular difference between tracks is 316 degrees. from question bank
explanation: Angular difference of 316° is equivalent to 44° (since 360-316=44) which is less than 45°, so they are considered same track.

question_text: The opposite tracks and intersecting tracks will be considered as reciprocal tracks if the angular difference of which is: from question bank

options.C: a) and b) both are correct

explanation: Reciprocal when angular difference >135° and <225°.

question_text: If the angular difference between tracks is 50 degrees than the tracks will be considered as: from question bank
explanation: 50° is between 45° and 135°, so crossing tracks.

question_text: Irrespective of the speeds and availability of navigational aids, the minimum longitudinal separation minima based on time for aircraft on same level and same tracks is: from question bank
explanation: Absolute minimum time separation is 15 minutes.

question_text: The minimum longitudinal separation minimum based on time for aircraft that have departed from the same aerodrome for same level and same tracks is 03 minutes provided: from question bank

options.A: Both aircraft are maintaining same speed
options.B: Both aircraft may not be maintaining same speed.
options.C: Preceding aircraft is maintaining TAS of 40 kts or faster.

explanation: 3 minutes separation applies when preceding is 40 kt faster.

question_text: When Nav aids permit frequent determination of speed and position, the minimum longitudinal separation based on time for aircraft at same level on crossing tracks is: from question bank
explanation: With frequent nav aid fixes, 10 minutes for crossing tracks.

question_text: BBC002 A300 Dacca / Dubai FL320 estimating Karachi VOR at 0635 via Chore, SVA752 A300 LAH / RYD FL320 estimating Karachi VOR at 0638 via NH, your action will be: from question bank
explanation: They are at same level and same point with 3 min separation; must change level of one aircraft, typically the later one.

question_text: For aircraft at the same level when preceding aircraft is maintaining 40 kts or more faster, the minimum longitudinal separation based on time for crossing tracks is: from question bank
explanation: When preceding is 40 kt faster, crossing track separation is 10 minutes.

question_text: PIA319 A310 F320 OPLA/OPKC reported MURLI 0253 UTC. PIA300 B747 KAR/ISL F330 MURLI 0255UTC. PIA 319 is requesting F340. PIA319 can be cleared to F340 at: from question bank
explanation: For same point, need 10 minutes separation for crossing? Actually PIA319 at 0253, PIA300 at 0255 – they are 2 min apart. To climb PIA319, we need to ensure separation. Typically 10 min between same point for same level? The answer key says 0304 UTC (11 min after 0253? Actually 0253+11=0304) – so likely 10 min + 1 min buffer.

question_text: PIA303 B747 LHR/KAR TAS 440 Kts maintaining F320, position 120 NM inbound to RK. SAI 234 B737 LHR/KAR TAS 400 Kts maintaining F300, positions 130 NM inbound to RK requesting F320. SAI 234 can be cleared to F320 by applying longitudinal separation based on distance using DME. from question bank
explanation: DME separation can be used if both on same DME station and distance separation meets minima.

question_text: In application of longitudinal separation based on distance using DME there is a clause Which states that each aircraft utilizes the same on track DME station. The term "on track" means: from question bank

options.A: Aircraft is flying directly inbound to the DME station.
options.B: Aircraft is flying directly outbound from the DME station
options.C: Both aircraft are flying either directly inbound to the DME station or directly outbound from the DME station.

explanation: On track means either all inbound or all outbound.

question_text: Application of longitudinal separation minimum with Mach number technique is only possible after the aircraft concerned have reported over the same reporting point. from question bank
explanation: Mach number technique requires common reporting point.

question_text: Longitudinal separation minimum with Mach number technique between two turbojet aircraft on same track, whether in level, climbing or descending shall be 07 min if the preceding aircraft is: from question bank
explanation: 0.04 Mach difference gives 7 min separation.

question_text: DME based separation between aircraft at same level and speed on crossing tracks is: from question bank
explanation: For crossing tracks at same speed, DME separation is 20 NM.

question_text: DME based separation between aircraft at same level on crossing tracks with preceding aircraft TAS 20 kts faster is: from question bank
explanation: If preceding is 20 kt faster, DME separation reduces to 10 NM.

question_text: Which of the following equipment enables frequent determination of position and speed of the aircraft? from question bank
explanation: VOR and DME together give position and speed; radar is not a navigational aid for position/speed determination.

question_text: PIA302 B747 KAR/LHR F330 estimate RK 0340. AIC187 A320 AMRATSAR / OMDB F340 estimate Rk 0350 with a request for F350. When AIC187 can be given climb to F360? from question bank

options.D: All a), b) and c) correct

explanation: AIC187 can climb to F360 after 0355 (10 min after PIA302 estimate) or after reporting RK, or before 0335 if before conflict.

question_text: The airspace around designated track which has been used for fuel dumping giving full allowance for vapour zone shall be treated reserved airspace for: from question bank
explanation: Fuel dumping area reserved for 15 minutes.

question_text: What is the lateral separation minimum between holding area and en-route aircraft? from question bank

options.A: 3 minutes flying time from the holding area.
options.B: 5 minutes flying time from the holding area.
options.C: 10 minutes flying time from the holding area.
options.D: 15 minutes flying time from the holding area.

explanation: 5 minutes separation between holding area and en-route traffic.

question_text: Minimum separation between departing aircraft from the same aerodrome is one minute when aircraft are to fly on tracks diverging by: from question bank
explanation: 1 minute separation requires divergence of at least 45 degrees.

question_text: Minimum separation between departing aircraft is one minute when aircraft are to fly on tracks diverging by 45 degrees. If approved by the appropriate ATS authority this minima may be reduced when aircraft are using: from question bank
explanation: Parallel runways may allow reduced separation.

question_text: Minimum take-off separation between two departing aircraft when preceding aircraft is more than 40 Kts faster than the following aircraft and both aircraft propose follow the same route is: from question bank
explanation: If preceding is >40 kt faster, 2 minutes.

question_text: What is the required separation if a departing aircraft will be flown through the level of preceding departing aircraft and both aircraft propose to follow the same track and the separation will be maintained or increased while levels are crossed. from question bank
explanation: 5 minutes when passing through level.

question_text: If an arriving aircraft is making a complete instrument approach, a departing aircraft may take-off in any direction until: from question bank

options.A: Arriving aircraft is 4 NM on final.
options.B: Arriving aircraft has started its procedure turn or base turn leading to final approach.
options.C: Arriving aircraft leaves its initial approach altitude for the instrument approach.

explanation: Departure may continue until arriving aircraft starts procedure turn or base turn.

question_text: Some of the longitudinal separation minima based on time or distance require that the preceding aircraft to maintain TAS of 20 kts or 40 kts faster than the succeeding aircraft at the time of application. How will you fulfill this restriction? from question bank

options.A: TAS is given in the flight plan.
options.C: Ascertain TAS from the flight crew at the time of application.

explanation: Must get actual TAS from flight crew at time of application.

question_text: If an arriving aircraft is making a straight-in approach, a departing aircraft may take-off in any direction: from question bank

options.A: 5 minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated over the instrument runway.
options.B: 15 minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated over the instrument runway.
options.C: 20 minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated over the instrument runway.
options.D: 25 minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated over the instrument runway.

explanation: 5 minutes before estimated time over threshold.

question_text: The ATC unit concerned shall not be required to apply wake turbulence separation for arriving VFR flights landing behind on the same runway as the preceding landing HEAVY or MEDIUM aircraft. from question bank
explanation: Wake turbulence separation may not be required for VFR if they maintain visual separation.

question_text: The ATC unit concerned shall not be required to apply wake turbulence separation between arriving IFR flights executing visual approach when it has reported the preceding aircraft in sight and has been instructed to follow and maintain own separation. from question bank
explanation: Visual separation with own separation maintains safety.

question_text: What is the longitudinal wake turbulence separation minima between light aircraft arriving behind a heavy aircraft; from question bank
explanation: Light behind Heavy: 3 minutes.

question_text: What is the longitudinal wake turbulence separation minimum between departing light aircraft behind a departing heavy aircraft when both aircraft use the same runway? from question bank
explanation: Departing Light behind Heavy: 2 minutes.

question_text: Longitudinal wake turbulence separation minimum between departing light aircraft behind a departing heavy aircraft when both aircraft use the same runway is two minutes or when the aircraft are using parallel runways separated by less than: from question bank
explanation: Parallel runways less than 760 m apart require 2 min.

question_text: What is the longitudinal wake turbulence separation minimum between LIGHT aircraft departing behind a MEDIUM aircraft from the same runway? from question bank
explanation: Light behind Medium: 2 minutes (for departure).

question_text: What is the longitudinal wake turbulence separation minimum between MEDIUM aircraft arriving behind a HEAVY aircraft? from question bank
explanation: Medium behind Heavy: 2 minutes (arriving).

question_text: Longitudinal wake turbulence separation minimum between a departing Medium aircraft behind a departing Heavy aircraft is three minutes when Medium aircraft is taking-off from: from question bank

options.A: Parallel runway less than 760 m apart.
options.B: An intermediate part of a parallel runway less 760 m apart.
options.C: An intermediate part of same runway.
options.D: Both b) and c) are correct.

explanation: 3 min required if taking off from intermediate point or parallel less than 760 m.

question_text: A situation in which in the opinion of a pilot or air traffic services personnel, the distance between the aircraft as well as their relative positions and speed have been such that the safety of the aircraft involved may have been compromised is known as: from question bank
explanation: Definition of aircraft proximity (AIRPROX).

question_text: An aircraft proximity in which the safety of the aircraft may have been compromised is classified as: from question bank
explanation: Safety not assured is the classification when safety may be compromised.

question_text: What is the wake turbulence minima between a Medium and a Heavy aircraft when Heavy aircraft is making a missed approach and the Medium aircraft is utilizing an opposite direction runway for take-off? from question bank
explanation: 2 minutes for opposite runway operations.

question_text: A procedural controller can give direct routing to an aircraft. from question bank
explanation: Procedural controller can issue direct routing if separation can be ensured.

question_text: A Pakistani registered aircraft hijacked at an airport in Pakistan or any foreign country is landing or has landed at an airport within Pakistan will be considered from hijacking category: from question bank
explanation: As per Pakistan procedures, category B.

question_text: Only controlled flights constitute essential traffic for each other when not separated by the appropriate separation minima. from question bank
explanation: Essential traffic is defined for controlled flights not separated.

question_text: A VFR flight would not constitute essential traffic to other VFR flight except in: from question bank
explanation: In Class B, VFR flights are separated from each other, so they are essential traffic.

question_text: The elevation of the highest point of the aerodrome is called: from question bank
explanation: Aerodrome elevation is the highest point elevation.

question_text: Separation minimum in the vicinity of aerodrome may be reduced by the aerodrome controller if adequate separation can be provided by this controller when: from question bank

options.A: He is confident of handling the traffic.
options.B: A superior officer is present in Control Tower.
options.C: Each aircraft is continuously visible to this controller.
options.D: Controller and pilot are known to each other.

explanation: Visibility of each aircraft is key for reduced separation.

question_text: Separation minimum in the vicinity of aerodrome may be reduced by the aerodrome controller if each aircraft is continuously visible to the flight crews of the other aircraft concerned and: from question bank

options.B: Pilots report that they can maintain their own separation.
options.C: Pilots report that they are visual with each other.
options.D: Pilots report that they are changing from IFR too VFR and visual with each other.

explanation: Pilots must report they can maintain own separation.

question_text: Separation minimum in the vicinity of aerodrome may be reduced by the aerodrome controller if one aircraft is following another and the flight crew of the succeeding aircraft reports that: from question bank

options.A: Preceding traffic is a light aircraft.
options.B: Preceding traffic is in sight and separation can be maintained.
options.C: Changing from IFR to VFR and field in sight.

explanation: Both B and D are required: in sight and ability to maintain separation.

question_text: Information on essential local traffic known to the controller shall be transmitted without delay to departing aircraft only. from question bank
explanation: Information should be transmitted to all affected aircraft, not just departing.

question_text: The air traffic services units should be when practicable advises aircraft operators or the designated representatives when anticipated delays are expected to exceed: from question bank
explanation: Delays exceeding 30 minutes should be advised.

question_text: The approach sequence shall established in a manner which will facilitate arrival of the maximum number of aircraft with: from question bank
explanation: Goal is least average delay.

question_text: If departures are delayed, the delayed flights shall normally be cleared in order based on their estimated time of departure, except that deviation from this order may be made to facilitate maximum number of departures with least average delay. from question bank
explanation: Order is based on ETD, but exceptions for efficiency.

question_text: In the event the aerodrome controller, after a take-off clearance or a landing clearance has been issued, becomes aware of a runway incursion or the imminent occurrence thereof, or the existence of any obstruction on or in close proximity to the runway likely to impair the safety of an aircraft taking off or landing, appropriate action shall be taken as follows: from question bank

options.A: cancel the take-off clearance for a departing aircraft
options.B: instruct a landing aircraft to execute a go-around or missed approach
options.C: in all cases inform the aircraft of the runway incursion or obstruction

explanation: All actions are appropriate.

question_text: An aircraft moving on a taxiway of an aerodrome is considered to be: from question bank
explanation: Aircraft on taxiway is part of air traffic.

question_text: When communication by a system of visual signals is deemed to be adequate, what will a signal of white flashes from Control Tower will mean to a vehicle on the runway? from question bank

options.B: Vacate the maneuvering area in accordance with local procedure.
options.C: Vacate the runway immediately and watch out for aircraft.

explanation: White flashes mean vacate maneuvering area per local procedure.

question_text: When the visual signals from control tower are not observed by the vehicle on the runway, what is the alternate way of getting the runway cleared? from question bank

options.A: Send another vehicle to the runway.
options.B: Ask the aircraft to make a low go.

explanation: Flashing runway/taxiway lights is alternate method.

question_text: Which of the following aircraft shall be given priority for landing? from question bank

options.C: Aircraft with shortage of crew members.

explanation: Hospital/medical emergency aircraft have priority.

question_text: A departing aircraft shall normally have the priority over the aircraft landing or in the final stages of an approach using the same or intersecting runway. from question bank
explanation: Landing aircraft normally have priority over departing.

question_text: In the interest to expedite traffic, if a clearance for immediate take-off is issued to an aircraft, the pilot of the aircraft shall: from question bank

options.A: Arrange take-off as early as possible.
options.D: Establish visual contact with the arrival.

explanation: Immediate take-off means one continuous movement without stopping.

question_text: Landing aircraft will not normally be permitted to cross the runway threshold on its final approach until the preceding departing aircraft has crossed the end of the runway-in-use, or has started a turn, or until all preceding landing aircraft are clear of the runway-in-use. from question bank
explanation: Standard runway separation.

question_text: Which of the following cannot be included in the essential information on aerodrome conditions: from question bank

options.A: Aircraft and vehicle operating on the manoeuvring area.
options.B: Construction or maintenance work on the manoeuvring area.
options.C: Rough or broken surfaces on a runway a taxiway.
options.D: Failure or irregular operation of part or all of the aerodrome lighting system.
options.E: All of the above can be included

explanation: All are essential information; the question asks what cannot be included, so the answer is E (none of the above are excluded).

question_text: Any or all VFR operation on or in the vicinity of aerodrome may be suspended by: from question bank

options.A: The approach control unit or the appropriate ACC

explanation: All three can suspend VFR operations.

question_text: An aircraft may not sustain a specified rate of climb or descent. The controller shall ensure that alternative methods of maintaining separation are applied in a timely manner. from question bank
explanation: If aircraft cannot comply, apply alternative separation.

question_text: What will you understand if in response to your instructions to an aircraft you receive this call from the aircraft? Unable, TCAS resolution advisory. from question bank

options.A: The pilot is unable to comply with TCAS resolution advisory.
options.B: The pilot is unable to comply with ATC instructions due TCAS resolution advisory.
options.C: The pilot is unable to comply with TCAS resolution advisory and ATC instruction.
options.D: None of the above is correct.

explanation: Pilot is following TCAS RA and cannot comply with ATC instruction.

question_text: What will a pilot understand when he receives this call from ATC. "RVR RUNWAY 36R 200m, 250m and 150 m" from question bank

options.C: RVR RWY 36R Stop end zone is 150m
options.D: All of the above are correct.

explanation: The three values correspond to touchdown, midpoint, and stop end zones respectively.

question_text: Which of the following is the call-sign for the Area Control Centre in Lahore? from question bank
explanation: Call-sign for ACC is 'Lahore Control'.

question_text: Which of the following is the call-sign for an Approach control office in Karachi? from question bank
explanation: Approach control call-sign is 'Karachi Approach'.

question_text: Which of the following is the call-sign for a Flight Information Centre Mumbai? from question bank
explanation: FIC call-sign is 'Mumbai Information'.

question_text: Which of the following is the call-sign for an Aeronautical Station Dushanbe? from question bank
explanation: Aeronautical station call-sign is 'Dushanbe Radio'.

question_text: Except in dire emergency, fuel dumping shall not be permitted: from question bank
explanation: Fuel dumping restricted over populated areas, thunderstorms, and holding areas.

question_text: Unless specially authorized by DG CAA, a flight shall not be cleared in compliance with VFR in Pakistan airspace: from question bank

options.C: Over the sea, more than 16 km from the coast for more than one hour.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

explanation: All three are restrictions on VFR.

question_text: Single engine aircraft are not permitted to fly over the high sea at a distance from where they cannot glide to the aerodrome. from question bank
explanation: The statement is false; single-engine aircraft may fly over sea if within gliding distance of land.

question_text: As per laid down VVIP procedures no aircraft shall be allowed to land or depart from an aerodrome or operate in the circuit of the aerodrome for the period: from question bank

options.A: 5 minutes before take-off till 5 minutes after take-off
options.B: 5 minutes before ETA till door close.
options.C: From 'DOOR CLOSE' to 5 minutes after take-off.
options.E: From door close to 15 minutes after take-off

explanation: VVIP restrictions apply from door close to 5 minutes after take-off.

question_text: As per laid down VVIP procedures, no aircraft shall be allowed to land or depart from an aerodrome or operate in the circuit of the aerodrome for the period: from question bank

options.A: 5 minutes before take-off till 5 minutes after take-off
options.B: 5 minutes before ETA till door open.
options.C: From 'DOOR CLOSE' to 15 minutes after take-off.
options.E: From door close to 5 minutes after take-off

explanation: For VVIP, restrictions apply from 5 min before ETA to door open.

question_text: All aircraft flying in Pakistan airspace are to keep listening watch on: from question bank
explanation: 121.5 MHz is the emergency frequency and listening watch is required.

question_text: No VFR operation shall be allowed during the period when the VVIP is expected to operate in: from question bank

options.C: In the block of 25 NM around VVIP aircraft in controlled airspace.
options.D: 1000 feet above or below the VVIP aircraft.

explanation: VVIP restrictions apply in controlled airspace.

question_text: When VVIP is flying in Pakistan no aircraft shall be cleared to operate in the block of 25 NM either side of intended route of the VVIP flight irrespective of type of airspace. from question bank
explanation: The block restriction may not apply to all airspace; often only controlled.

question_text: When RA is initiated and pilot deviates from ATC clearance, he is not considered to be violating ATC instructions. from question bank
explanation: Pilot following TCAS RA is not considered in violation.

question_text: Except when a clearance is obtained from ATC unit, a VFR flight shall not take-off or land within a control zone or enter in aerodrome traffic pattern when: from question bank

options.A: Ground visibility is less than 8 Km.
options.B: Ground visibility is less than 5 Km.
options.C: Ground visibility is less than 3 Km.
options.D: Ground visibility is less than 1.5 Km.

explanation: VFR requires ground visibility ≥5 km.

question_text: Except when necessary for take-off or landing, or except by permission from the Director General, CAA, a VFR flight shall not be flown Over the congested areas of cities, towns or settlements or over an open-air assembly of persons at a height less than _______ above the highest obstacle within a radius of 600 m from the aircraft; from question bank
explanation: Minimum height over congested areas is 1000 ft above obstacles.

question_text: Except necessary for take-off or landing a VFR flight shall not operate over the congested area of cities, town or settlement at a height less than 1000 feet else where not less than: from question bank
explanation: Elsewhere minimum height is 500 ft.

question_text: Two aircraft at the same track and the same level, if succeeding aircraft with TAS 450 Kts and preceding aircraft is TAS 490 Kts. The first aircraft departed at 1835, the 2nd aircraft can be cleared to depart at: from question bank
explanation: Preceding is 40 kt faster, so 2 minutes separation required, so 1835 + 2 = 1837.

question_text: A VFR flight below F150 may be authorized to enter a control zone for landing or depart on a direct track in IMC conditions provided that: from question bank

options.A: Only one aircraft or formation of aircraft is cleared for a specific period.
options.B: No other traffic is operating or expected to operate within the control zone.
options.C: Ground visibility is not less than 1.5 km.

explanation: All conditions must be met for special VFR.

question_text: An IFR flight shall relay estimates and flight level over the FIR boundary prior to entry into FIR at least by: from question bank
explanation: 15 minutes prior to boundary.

question_text: Only IFR flights are permitted in: from question bank
explanation: Class A is IFR only.

question_text: In which classification of airspace IFR and VFR flights are permitted and are separated from each other. from question bank
explanation: In Class B, IFR and VFR are separated.

question_text: In which classification of airspace VFR flights are separated from IFR flights and receive traffic information in respect of other VFR flights. from question bank
explanation: In Class C, VFR separated from IFR, and receive traffic info on other VFR.

question_text: "IFR and VFR flights are permitted and all flights are subject to Air Traffic Control service. IFR flights are separated from other IFR flights and receive traffic information in respect of VFR flights. VFR flights receive traffic information in respect of all other flights". Name the classification of airspace. from question bank
explanation: Description matches Class D.

question_text: In class E airspace IFR flights are separated from other IFR flights. from question bank
explanation: Class E provides separation between IFR flights.

question_text: In which classification of airspace IFR flights receive Advisory service in respect of other IFR flights. from question bank
explanation: Class F provides advisory service.

question_text: IFR and VFR flights are permitted and receive flight information service if requested. Name the classification of airspace: from question bank
explanation: Class G provides FIS on request.

question_text: SIGMET information passed to the aircraft should cover a portion of the route up to the flying of: from question bank
explanation: SIGMET covers up to two hours of flight.

question_text: In Pakistan airspace the revised ATS route structure is based on RNAV. from question bank
explanation: Pakistan has implemented RNAV-based routes.

question_text: Aircraft on Int'l flights proceeding to Afghanistan from Pakistan should be: from question bank
explanation: Q-3.201 answer key indicates RNP 10 required for Afghanistan flights.

question_text: Pilots of aircraft meeting RNP type requirements shall indicate this at item 10 of the Flight Plan as: from question bank
explanation: Item 10 uses 'R' to indicate RNP capability (e.g., R for RNP approved).

question_text: In Karachi and Lahore FIR, airspace outside the control areas and outside the ATS routes, irrespective of vertical limits, the classification of airspace is G. from question bank
explanation: Outside CTA and ATS routes, airspace is Class G.

question_text: Karachi and Lahore FIR airspace is prescribed as RVSM airspace between F290 to F410 inclusive, in: from question bank

options.B: Control Areas and ATS routes above F155.

explanation: RVSM applies in controlled airspace from F290-F410.

question_text: In Pakistan visual climb/descent is permitted for IFR flights below F 150 in VMC in class A, B and C airspace. from question bank
explanation: Visual climb/descent is not permitted in Class A, B, C; only in D and E maybe.

question_text: Two aircraft are considered to be on reciprocal track when the angular difference between their tracks is: from question bank

options.C: Less than 135° and more than 45° or less than 315° and more than 225°

explanation: Reciprocal when angular difference >135° and <225°.

question_text: Procedures for RVSM require that to operate in RVSM airspace an aircraft shall have two primary altimeter systems, one automatic altitude keeping device, one altitude operating transponder and one: from question bank
explanation: RVSM requires an altitude alerting device.

question_text: When operating in RVSM airspace an RVSM compliant aircraft must inform ATC whenever: from question bank

options.A: No longer RVSM compliant due equipment failure.
options.B: Experience loss of redundancy of altimetry system.
options.C: Encounter turbulence that can affect the capability to maintain the flight level.

explanation: All are reportable events.

question_text: During the cleared transition between levels an RVSM approved aircraft should not over shoot the assigned flight level by more than: from question bank
explanation: Overshoot limit is 150 ft.

question_text: An RVSM aircraft should be given priority for levels allocation over Non-RVSM approved aircraft. from question bank
explanation: RVSM aircraft have priority for level allocation.

question_text: Non-RVSM approved aircraft may be cleared to climb to and operate above F410 or descend to and operate below F290 under certain conditions. from question bank
explanation: Non-RVSM can operate outside RVSM airspace.

question_text: All the civilian aircraft are fitted with emergency VHF frequency 121.5MHZ. from question bank
explanation: Civilian aircraft are required to carry 121.5 MHz.

question_text: Non-RVSM approved aircraft being utilized on humanitarian purpose may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace. from question bank
explanation: Humanitarian flights may be exempted.

question_text: A non-RVSM approved aircraft may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace if it is transporting an spare engine mounted under its wings. from question bank
explanation: Aircraft with external stores may be exempted.

question_text: A non-RVSM aircraft shall not be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace even if the aircraft was RVSM approved and has experienced an equipment failure and is being flown to a maintenance facility for repair in order to meet RVSM requirement. from question bank
explanation: May be cleared if flown to maintenance facility.

question_text: A non-RVSM approved aircraft may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace if it is an state aircraft. from question bank
explanation: State aircraft may be exempted.

question_text: Non-RVSM approved aircraft owned by Military or Police may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace at RVSM levels. from question bank
explanation: Military/Police may be cleared.

question_text: Non-RVSM approved aircraft of Military, Custom and Police may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace keeping RVSM separation. from question bank
explanation: If not RVSM approved, they cannot be separated using RVSM minima.

question_text: A Non-RVSM approved State-aircraft may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace. The status of the aircraft shall be mentioned in the ICAO approved flight plan in item: from question bank
explanation: Item 18 is for other information, including STS/.

question_text: A Non-RVSM approved Police aircraft may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace. The status of the aircraft shall be mentioned in the ICAO approved flight plan in item 18 as: from question bank
explanation: Use STS/Police in Item 18.

question_text: If in Karachi or Lahore FIR greater than moderate turbulence are reported by the pilot than RVSM operation may be suspended in the affected area only. from question bank
explanation: Turbulence may cause suspension of RVSM.

question_text: Essential information on aerodrome shall include information relating to: from question bank

options.A: Water on a runway, a taxiway or an apron,
options.B: Failure or irregular operation of part or all of the aerodrome lighting system
options.C: Work in progress on the terminal building outside apron

explanation: Essential info includes runway/taxiway/apron conditions and lighting; terminal building work outside apron is not essential.

question_text: An RVSM aircraft must have functioning ACAS on board. from question bank
explanation: RVSM does not require ACAS; only altimetry and altitude alerting.

question_text: Which frequency has been allotted for inter pilot communication. from question bank
explanation: 123.45 MHz is for air-to-air communication.

question_text: If an RVSM approved aircraft when flying in RVSM airspace, is unsure of its vertical position due degradation of primary altimeter system then the pilot of the aircraft should: from question bank

options.C: Broadcast position, flight level and intentions on 121.5Mhz
options.D: All of the above are correct.

explanation: All actions are required.

question_text: When flying in RVSM airspace if a pilot reports unsure of vertical position due to degradation of primary altimetry systems and requests to continue in RVSM airspace. ATC may accommodate the aircraft if: from question bank

options.C: Conventional vertical separation can be provided.

explanation: Any of these separation methods may be used.

question_text: When flying in RVSM airspace if a pilot reports failure of all altitude control system and requests to continue in RVSM airspace, ATC may accommodate this aircraft if: from question bank

options.C: Conventional vertical separation can be provided.

explanation: Same as previous.

question_text: When flying in RVSM airspace, if a pilot reports loss of redundancy in primary altimetry systems, and requests to continue in RVSM airspace. Aircraft can be permitted to operate in RVSM airspace under certain conditions. from question bank
explanation: May be permitted with separation.

question_text: A circle of radius of 13,000 feet measured from the reference point. Within this area obstructions are to be limited to a height of 150 feet above level of the reference point. According to the National Airfield Clearance Policy the above surface is known as: from question bank
explanation: Inner Horizontal Surface has radius 13,000 ft and height limit 150 ft.

question_text: When flying in RVSM airspace a pilot reports turbulence (greater than moderate) which may impact the aircraft's capability to maintain a flight level. The aircraft may continue to operate in RVSM airspace provided: from question bank

options.C: Conventional vertical separation can be provided.

explanation: All separation methods may be used.

question_text: Local flights at an uncontrolled aerodrome outside control zone may not submit a flight plan provided such flights operate below: from question bank
explanation: Below 1000 ft, local flights may not need flight plan.

question_text: A flight wholly conducted in the vicinity of an aerodrome i.e. take-off from an aerodrome, fly in the circuit and land back. Such a flight is called. from question bank
explanation: Definition of local flight.

question_text: Flight plan for a flight is accepted before ETD within: from question bank
explanation: Accepted up to 2 hours before ETD.

question_text: Flight Plan must be submitted at least 30 minutes before for: from question bank

options.D: Both a) and c) are correct.

explanation: IFR and international VFR require 30 min advance.

question_text: In Pakistan airspace it is mandatory for turbojet aircraft to carry ACAS if its maximum certified take-off mass is: from question bank
explanation: ACAS required for turbojet > 15000 kg.

question_text: In Pakistan airspace it is mandatory for turbojet aircraft to carry ACAS if the aircraft is carrying: from question bank
explanation: ACAS required if carrying 30 passengers or more.

question_text: In Pakistan serviceable transponder including Mode C is mandatory in controlled airspace above: from question bank
explanation: Transponder with Mode C required above FL250.

question_text: In Karachi, Lahore CTA and Cherat Control Zone, the carriage of serviceable transponder is mandatory for all aircraft. from question bank
explanation: All aircraft require transponder in those areas.

question_text: No scheduled flight shall be cleared to depart if delay exceeds 06 hours of its scheduled ETD unless cleared by concerned ATS directorate. This rule shall not apply if the delay is due to: from question bank

options.D: All of the above are correct.

explanation: All are valid reasons for delay beyond 6 hours.

question_text: A notice containing information that does not qualify for the origination of a NOTAM or for inclusion in the AIP, but which relates to flight safety, air navigation, technical, administrative or legislative matters is known as: from question bank
explanation: Definition of AIC.

question_text: Which of the following will constitute essential traffic? from question bank

options.A: Any flight not separated in Class A and B airspace;
options.B: IFR flights not separated in Class C, D and E airspace;
options.C: IFR flights not separated from VFR flights in Class C airspace;

explanation: All are essential traffic situations.

question_text: Essential traffic information shall include: from question bank

options.B: Cruising level of the aircraft concerned.
options.C: Estimate of the reporting point nearest to the point of confliction.

explanation: All three are included.

question_text: COSPAS SARSAT is a satellite system designed to provide distress alert and location data to KC RCC for 406MHZ beacons activated: from question bank
explanation: COSPAS-SARSAT provides data to Pakistan RCC for beacons activated in Pakistan.

question_text: Mach number technique shall not be applied if cruise phase of a flight is less than two hour. from question bank
explanation: No such restriction; it is applied regardless of cruise length.

question_text: Mach number technique may be applied to aircraft which propose to follow diverging or converging tracks. from question bank
explanation: Mach number technique is for same track only.

question_text: What shall be the separation using Mach Number technique if the following aircraft maintains a Mach number .02 greater than the preceding aircraft and the distance to cover is 1200 NM? from question bank
explanation: For 0.02 Mach difference and distance 1200 NM, separation is 14 minutes.

question_text: What shall be the separation using Mach Number technique if the following aircraft maintains a Mach number .02 greater than the preceding aircraft and the distance to cover is 1800 NM? from question bank
explanation: For 0.02 Mach difference and 1800 NM, separation is 16 minutes.

question_text: Which of the following is not a content of ATC clearance? from question bank

explanation: Spoilers increase drag and reduce lift; they can reduce speed but are primarily for lift dump and roll control.

question_text: Spoilers are used after landing to enable the aircraft to stop in a comparatively short distance. from question bank
explanation: Spoilers increase drag and reduce lift, aiding braking.

question_text: Aircraft which have double wings are called biplanes. from question bank
explanation: Biplanes have two wings.

question_text: A flight which is subject to an ATC Clearance is called a: from question bank
explanation: Definition of controlled flight.

question_text: When so requested by an aircraft and provided it is agreed by the pilot of the other aircraft and so authorized by the appropriate ATS authority an ATC unit may clear a controlled flight in VMC during the hours of day light to fly subject to maintaining own separation to one other aircraft remaining in VMC operating in: from question bank

options.C: Airspace Classes A, B and C

explanation: Own separation is allowed in Class D and E.

question_text: The lateral limit of CTR shall extend to at least: from question bank
