options.B: 5 NM from the centre of the aerodrome or aerodromes in the direction from which the approaches may be made.
options.C: 15 NM from the centre of the aerodrome.
options.D: 25 NM from the centre of the aerodrome.

explanation: CTR lateral limit is at least 5 NM in approach direction.

question_text: If a control zone is located within lateral limits of a control area it shall extend upwards from: from question bank

options.B: 1000 feet above the highest obstruction in control zone.
options.C: Surface of the earth ( ground or water )
options.D: Any level determined by the appropriate authority.

explanation: Control zone extends from surface.

question_text: The lower limit of control area shall be established at a height above ground or water of not less then: from question bank
explanation: Lower limit of control area is 700 ft AGL.

question_text: Alerting service shall be provided to: from question bank

options.A: All aircraft provided with Air Traffic Control Service.
options.B: In so far as practicable to all other aircraft having filed a flight plan or otherwise known to the air traffic services.
options.C: To any aircraft known or believed to be the subject of unlawful interference.

explanation: Alerting service provided to all those.

question_text: A VFR flight can request F 65 provided it proposes to fly on track: from question bank
explanation: VFR levels: for tracks 180-359, odd levels +500? Actually F65 is for tracks 180-359? Standard VFR: eastbound (000-179) odd thousands +500? Actually ICAO: VFR below FL150 use +500 ft. For F65, that's 6500 ft, which is for westbound tracks (180-359).

question_text: N9002 estimates position A at 1232 UTC. There is no communication with the aircraft since 1224 UTC. The aircraft will be considered overdue or in uncertainty phase at: from question bank
explanation: Uncertainty phase starts 30 minutes after ETA or time of last communication? Actually if no communication since 1224, and ETA at 1232, then after 30 min from ETA? The answer key says D (1224) - maybe because no communication for 8 minutes before ETA? Actually the rule: uncertainty phase when aircraft fails to report within 30 minutes after ETA or 30 min after last communication. Since last communication at 1224, 30 min later is 1254. But options don't include that. The answer key says D (1224) - possibly they consider overdue at the time communication was lost? I'll follow answer key.

question_text: VOR transmitter operates on VHF band from 108 to 118 MHz. from question bank
explanation: VOR frequency range is 108-118 MHz.

question_text: The entry into the holding pattern shall be according to the bearing. from question bank
explanation: Entry is according to heading, not bearing.

question_text: Generally the effect of jet stream on the estimates of aircraft remains from: from question bank

options.A: From end of November to end of March.
options.B: From end of October to end of January.
options.C: From end of December to end of February.

explanation: Jet stream effects are from November to March.

question_text: A special geographical location used to define an area navigation route or the flight path of an aircraft employing area navigation is called a way point. from question bank
explanation: Definition of waypoint.

question_text: The phrase cleared via flight plan route shall not be used unless the flight Plan has been read. from question bank
explanation: Must have read the flight plan before using that phrase.

question_text: AIRPROX is a code word used in an air traffic incident report to designate aircraft proximity. from question bank
explanation: Definition of AIRPROX.

question_text: The relevant authority designated by the state responsible for providing air traffic services in the airspace concerned is known as: from question bank
explanation: Appropriate ATS authority is defined.

question_text: Which of the following will be considered as an essential local traffic to an aircraft? from question bank

options.A: A vehicle close to maneuvering area which constitutes hazard to the concerned aircraft.
options.B: Personnel crossing close to runway, which constitute hazard to the concerned aircraft.
options.C: Any other aircraft constituting hazard to the concerned aircraft.

explanation: All are essential local traffic.

question_text: The range over which the pilot of an aircraft on the center line of runway can see the runway surface markings or lights delineating the runway or identifying its center line is known as: from question bank
explanation: Definition of RVR.

question_text: IFR and VFR flights are permitted. IFR flights are provided with Air Traffic Control Service and are separated from other IFR flights. All flights receive traffic information as far as is practical. The classification of airspace is: from question bank
explanation: Class E: IFR separated from IFR, all receive traffic info as far as practicable.

question_text: An aircraft stand should provide the following minimum clearance between aircraft using the stand and any adjacent building, aircraft on another stand and other object with code letter E. from question bank
explanation: Code E requires 7.5 m clearance.

question_text: The lateral and vertical limits of aerodrome traffic zone are 5 NM around and 2000 ft above from the ground or water. from question bank
explanation: ATZ limits are 5 NM and 2000 ft AGL.

question_text: PAPI's shall be operated: from question bank

options.C: During night and during day in poor visibility.
options.D: During day and night irrespective of visibility and weather conditions.

explanation: PAPI operated day and night regardless of visibility.

question_text: ATS route G202 indicates that: from question bank

options.A: It is in class A airspace.
options.C: It forms a part of regional network.

explanation: Routes with letter G are regional (non-RNAV).

question_text: Unless otherwise instructed all turns in the standard race track holding pattern shall be made: from question bank

options.C: In accordance with the entry pattern.

explanation: Standard holding pattern turns right.

question_text: Angular difference between direction of True north and Magnetic north at any given place is called: from question bank
explanation: Variation is the angle between true north and magnetic north.

question_text: A collision of a vehicle with an aircraft parked on a stand with no person on board will be considered as an accident. from question bank
explanation: Without injury or substantial damage, it may be an incident, not accident.

question_text: The time at which the ATC expects that an arriving aircraft following a delay will leave the holding pattern to complete its approach for a landing is known as: from question bank
explanation: EAT is Expected Approach Time.

question_text: The estimated time at which an aircraft will commence its movement associated with departure is called estimated elapsed time. from question bank
explanation: That is estimated off-block time (EOBT), not EET.

question_text: Information on essential local traffic known to the controller should be transmitted to departing and arriving aircraft when practicable. from question bank
explanation: The earlier answer says false? Actually Q-3.412 in original says False. So follow that.

question_text: The estimated time required to proceed from one significant point to another is known as Estimated Elapsed Timed. from question bank
explanation: EET is estimated elapsed time.

question_text: For IFR flights, the time at which it is estimated that the aircraft will arrive over that designated point, defined by reference to navigation aids, from which it is intended that an instrument approach procedure will be commenced is called: from question bank
explanation: That is Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA).

question_text: The time at which it is estimated that a VFR flight will arrive over the aerodrome is called its: from question bank
explanation: VFR ETA is over the aerodrome.

question_text: For IFR flights, if no navigation aid is associated with the aerodrome, the time at which the aircraft will arrive over the aerodrome is called its: from question bank
explanation: ETA over aerodrome.

question_text: Specified information provided to air traffic services units, relative to an intended flight or portion of a flight of an aircraft is called: from question bank
explanation: Definition of flight plan.

question_text: Unsynchronized transponder responses received by a ground interrogator unit triggered by interrogation signals from other ground units are known as: from question bank
explanation: FRUIT (False Replies Unsynchronized In Time) are unsynchronized responses from other interrogators.

question_text: False codes displayed as the result of a SSR Radar interrogator's inability to differentiate between the individual response codes of two or more aircraft closer together in space than the minimum acceptable to the equipment are known as: from question bank
explanation: Garble occurs when replies from close aircraft overlap, causing false codes.

question_text: The level of unwanted signals coming into the receiver from the aerial or generated within the receiver itself is known as: from question bank
explanation: Noise is internal or external unwanted signal.

question_text: The direction of the plane of the electric field of an electromagnetic wave is known as: from question bank
explanation: Polarization is the orientation of the electric field.

question_text: Visual indication, in non symbolic form, on a radar display of the position of an aircraft obtained by primary or secondary radar is known as: from question bank
explanation: Radar blip is the non-symbolic indication.

question_text: The visual indication on a radar display of a radar signal reflected from an object is known as: from question bank
explanation: Radar echo is the reflected signal indication.

question_text: In the Radar display the top most line of display is called: from question bank
explanation: The top line is the General Information Area.

question_text: For surveillance radar approach rate of descent for 3 degree glide path is calculated by: from question bank
explanation: Rate of descent (ft/min) ≈ 5 × Ground speed (kts) for 3° glide path.

question_text: In SSR interrogation transmission are made on a frequency of 1090 MHz. from question bank
explanation: SSR interrogations are on 1030 MHz; replies on 1090 MHz.

question_text: In a transponder reply spacing between the second framing pulse and SPI pulse is: from question bank
explanation: SPI pulse occurs 4.35 µs after the second framing pulse.

question_text: When echoes beyond the normal range of radar are seen on a radar display. This phenomenon is known as: from question bank
explanation: Anomalous propagation causes echoes beyond normal range due to atmospheric conditions.

question_text: Anomalous propagation is caused due to: from question bank
explanation: Meteorological conditions (temperature inversions, humidity) cause anomalous propagation.

question_text: An aircraft speed measured along the sweep, at which, Radar with MTI cannot detect an aircraft is known as: from question bank
explanation: Blind velocity occurs when the target's radial velocity causes cancellation in MTI.

question_text: Radar echoes from ground objects, terrain and clouds are known as: from question bank
explanation: Clutter is unwanted echoes from terrain, weather, etc.

question_text: Circular polarization is useful for: from question bank

options.A: Canceling the returns from unwanted object.
options.B: Canceling the returns from ground object
options.C: Canceling the returns from circular object
options.D: Canceling the returns from stationary objects

explanation: Circular polarization helps cancel returns from symmetrical (circular) objects like raindrops.

question_text: An electronic device used in Radar, which cancel the returns from stationary objects isknown as: from question bank
explanation: MTI (Moving Target Indicator) cancels stationary clutter.

question_text: The speed of the aircraft directly read from Air Speed Indicator is known as: from question bank
explanation: IAS is the direct reading from the airspeed indicator.

question_text: IAS corrected for position and instrument error is known as: from question bank
explanation: CAS is IAS corrected for position and instrument error.

question_text: The speed at which an aircraft passes through the air is known as: from question bank
explanation: TAS is the actual speed relative to the air mass.

question_text: The speed of the air at which it is passing over the surface of the aircraft, will be the true airspeed of the aircraft. from question bank
explanation: TAS is the speed of the aircraft relative to the air, equal to the speed of air over the surface.

question_text: The speed of the aircraft expressed as a ratio of True Air Speed to the local speed of sound is known as: from question bank
explanation: Mach number is TAS divided by speed of sound.

question_text: The True airspeed of a descending aircraft will increase if the aircraft maintains constant Indicated airspeed: from question bank
explanation: Descending to lower altitude increases air density, so TAS decreases for constant IAS.

question_text: The color of a VFR flight observe in the display: from question bank
explanation: VFR flights are displayed in yellow on the radar display.

question_text: The alarm indicator "PR" normally appears on the display at: from question bank
explanation: PR (Pre-Runway?) appears 120 seconds before? Actually according to answer key, it's 120 seconds.

question_text: Echoes caused by pockets of air or flock of birds are known as: from question bank
explanation: Angels are radar returns from birds, insects, or atmospheric inhomogeneities.

question_text: Radar control service is provided outside controlled airspace along the ATS routes in Karachi and Lahore FIR's above: from question bank
explanation: Radar service outside controlled airspace is provided above FL155.

question_text: Within collocated primary and secondary Terminal Approach Radar of Karachi, Lahore and Islamabad, the radar separation up to a height of F255 is: from question bank

options.D: 5 NM in all direction except for reciprocal traffic.

explanation: Within TMA radar coverage, separation is 5 NM up to FL255.

question_text: Radar separation using SSR position symbol of transponder equipped aircraft is 15 NM while aircraft are on: from question bank
explanation: 15 NM separation applies for all track types when using SSR position symbols.

question_text: Where lateral separation is not established vertical separation shall be maintained between transponder equipped aircraft on reciprocal tracks outside coverage of Terminal Approach Radars from the time their SSR position symbol before passing each other are apart by: from question bank
explanation: For reciprocal tracks, vertical separation required when symbols are 50 NM apart before passing.

question_text: Where lateral separation is not established vertical separation shall be maintained between transponder equipped aircraft on reciprocal tracks outside coverage of Terminal Approach Radars from the time their SSR position symbol before passing are apart by 50 NM until they have passed each other and are apart by: from question bank
explanation: After passing, separation can be released when symbols are 5 NM apart.

question_text: Out side Terminal Approach Radar of Karachi, Lahore and Islamabad, when the progress of the transponder equipped aircraft on same or crossing tracks whether at same level, climbing or descending is continuously monitored on radar, the longitudinal separation is: from question bank

options.A: 05 minutes and distance between these aircraft is not less than 30 NM.
options.B: 05 minutes and distance between these aircraft is not less than 50 NM.
options.C: 05 minutes and distance between these aircraft is not less than 60 NM.
options.D: 20 minutes and distance between these aircraft is not less than 30 NM.

explanation: Longitudinal separation: 5 minutes and 30 NM distance.

question_text: When the radar based spacing required for the avoidance of turbulent wake differ from the spacing required for traffic separation, which spacing shall be applied: from question bank

options.B: Separation minima required for air traffic
options.C: The larger of the two shall be applied

explanation: The larger separation must be applied.

question_text: When providing vectors to IFR flights, level assigned by the radar controller shall provide minimum terrain clearance. from question bank
explanation: IFR vectors must ensure terrain clearance.

question_text: When ATC provides vectors to VFR flights, the controller retains the responsibility of terrain clearance. from question bank
explanation: VFR pilots are responsible for terrain clearance even when vectored.

question_text: Radar controller shall always provide accurate information of weather cells to the aircraft. from question bank
explanation: Radar may not always detect weather accurately; pilot weather radar is primary.

question_text: Radar controller may not agree to provide radar vectors round the weather because of other operational priorities. from question bank
explanation: Other priorities may preclude vectoring for weather.

explanation: Operational priorities may prevent vectoring.

question_text: Visual indication on a radar display of unwanted signals is called: from question bank
explanation: Clutter is unwanted signals.

question_text: The situation which exists when the radar blip or radar position symbol of a particular aircraft is seen and identified on radar display is known as: from question bank
explanation: Radar contact is when the aircraft is seen and identified.

question_text: The situation which exists when the radar position of a particular aircraft is seen on a radar-display and positively identified by the air traffic controller is known as: from question bank
explanation: Radar identification is positive identification.

question_text: AT rate one turn, 360 degrees will be completed in: from question bank

question_text: The vertical speed of the aircraft shown in: from question bank
explanation: Vertical speed is shown in Line2.

question_text: How does the Ground Speed calculated for the calculation of rate of descent in SRA. from question bank

options.A: Head wind component is subtracted to the True Air Speed.
options.B: Tail wind component is added from the True Air Speed.
options.C: Both a) and b) are correct.

question_text: Total numbers of codes available in 12 information pulses of transponder reply are: from question bank
explanation: 12 pulses give 2^12 = 4096 codes.

question_text: Which of the following formula is correct to compute the altitude at each NM on final in surveillance radar approach for a 3 degree glide path? from question bank

options.A: 106 x 3 (angle of glide slope) x distance from touch down + Aerodrome elevation.
options.B: 100 x 3 (angle of glide slope) x distance from touch down + Aerodrome elevation should be rounded to nearest 100 of feet.
options.C: 106 x 3 (angle of glide slope) x distance from touch down + Aerodrome elevation. ( altitude obtained should be rounded to nearest 100 of feet)

explanation: Formula: Altitude = 106 × 3 × DME + elevation, rounded to nearest 100 ft.

question_text: Which of the following is an incorrect action of the radar controller in case of radar failure? from question bank

options.B: Establish vertical separation of 500 ft in critical cases.
options.C: Inform aircraft of the radar failure.

question_text: Radar echoes from vehicles, trains, birds, meteorological phenomena etc are known as: from question bank
explanation: These are angels.

question_text: The Minimum Safe Altitude Warning (MSAW) is defined in adaptation and it is activated/inhibited by the CMD. When it is activated, the icon text colour is YELLOW and, when it is inhibited, the icon text colour turns to WHITE. from question bank
explanation: Description matches system behavior.

question_text: Blind speed, tangential fade and concentric fade are the disadvantages of a suppressor known as: from question bank
explanation: MTI has disadvantages like blind speeds.

question_text: The minimum range at which a target can be detected depends on: from question bank

options.D: All of the above are correct.

explanation: All factors affect minimum range.

question_text: The minimum distance between two targets on the same bearing which can exist and still make it possible to distinguish between the targets on a SDD of radar is known as: from question bank
explanation: Range resolution is the minimum distance along the same bearing.

question_text: The pulse recurrence frequency is the: from question bank

options.A: The number pulses produced in one second.
options.B: The number pulses produced in one minute.
options.C: The number pulses produced in one hour.

explanation: PRF is pulses per second.

question_text: The minimum angular separation between two targets at the same range which can exist and still make it possible to distinguish between the targets on the SDD of radar is known as: from question bank
explanation: Azimuth resolution is the minimum angular separation.

question_text: In the radar display the transfer of radar identity (hand over) appears close to the: from question bank

options.A: Change over point or when required

question_text: What is the disadvantage of the use of MTI in Primary radar? from question bank

options.A: Targets going away from radar head will be lost.

question_text: The blind speed is one of the disadvantages of MTI. Which of the following method can be used to minimize the effects of the blind speeds? from question bank

options.C: Both a) and b) are correct.

explanation: Staggering or varying PRF helps mitigate blind speeds.

question_text: Which of the following is not included in the basic components of Radar? from question bank

question_text: Which of the following is not included in the basic components of Radar? from question bank

question_text: Which of the following is a limitation of primary radar? from question bank
explanation: All are limitations.

question_text: In SSR system the ground transmissions or interrogations are composed of pairs of pulse signals as P1 and P3. The determination of mode of interrogation is done by: from question bank

options.A: The spacing between P1 and P3.
options.C: The frequency of the interrogation signals

question_text: In SSR system the ground transmissions or interrogations are composed of pairs of pulse signals known as P1 and P3. explanation: Mode A spacing is 8 µs.

question_text: In SSR system the ground transmissions or interrogations are composed of pairs of pulse signals known as P1 and P3. The determination of mode of interrogation is done by the specific spacing between P1 and P3. The spacing between P1 and P3 in Military mode 3 is: from question bank
explanation: Mode 3 is same as Mode A (8 µs).

question_text: In SSR system the ground transmissions or interrogations are composed of pairs of pulse signals known as P1 and P3. explanation: Both are 8 µs.

question_text: In civil Mode A and Military Mode 3 the spacing between the ground transmitted interrogation signals P1 and P3 is same. This compatibility achieves civil and military common usage and is referred to as mode 3/A. from question bank
explanation: Correct.

question_text: The reply from the transponder consists of two framing pluses F1 and F2. The spacing between these two framing pulses is for all modes of interrogation. from question bank
explanation: F1-F2 spacing is 20.3 µs.

question_text: Speed control should not be applied to the aircraft on final approach after passing: from question bank
explanation: Speed control not after 4 NM on final.

question_text: Which information is missing from the following Radar handover? "Radar handover PIA301 squawking 1033 Flight level 360." from question bank

options.D: Wake turbulence category of the aircraft.

question_text: To request termination of pressure altitude transmission because of faulty operation which of the following phraseology is correct? from question bank

options.C: SWITCH OFF MODE CHARLIE DUE WRONG INDICATION.
options.D: PUT OFF MODE CHARLIE WRONG INDICATION.

explanation: Correct phraseology: STOP SQUAWK CHARLIE WRONG INDICATION.

question_text: Which of the following phraseology is correct when you want an aircraft to switch on Mode C? from question bank
explanation: Standard phrase: SQUAWK CHARLIE.

question_text: At Karachi, Lahore and Islamabad TMA, PSR and SSR are collocated and radar separation is 5NM. Radar services will continue to be provided in case the PSR fails. from question bank
explanation: SSR can still provide separation if PSR fails.

question_text: At Karachi, Lahore and Islamabad TMA, PSR and SSR are collocated and radar separation is 5NM. Radar services will still be provided in case the PSR fails but the radar separation will change from 5 NM to 15 NM. from question bank

question_text: Which of the following is not an essential requirement in the provision of radar service? from question bank
explanation: Direct communication is essential; radar map and Mode A are not always required.

question_text: Number of aircraft simultaneously provided with the radar service depends upon certain factors. Which of the following factors may not be considered in this regard? from question bank

options.D: Type of radar service to be provided.

explanation: Seniority is not a factor.

question_text: Number of aircraft simultaneously provided with the radar service depends upon certain factors. Which of the following factors may not be considered in this regard? from question bank
explanation: Airspace classification is not a direct factor; others are.

question_text: Which of the following codes has been reserved internationally for use by the pilot entering in a state of emergency? from question bank
explanation: 7700 is emergency.

question_text: Adherence of which of the following is required by the controllers and pilots to ensure the safe and efficient use of SSR? from question bank
explanation: All are required.

question_text: The tolerance value used to determine that Mode C-derived level information displayed to the controller in RVSM Airspace shall be: from question bank

question_text: Verification of accuracy of Mode C-derived information displayed to the controller shall be effected at least once by suitably equipped ATS unit: from question bank

options.B: As soon as possible if not done on initial contact.
options.C: On initial contact and before radar handover.
options.D: Both a) and b) are correct.

explanation: Verification on initial contact, or as soon as possible.

question_text: The pilot of the aircraft whose Mode C-derived level information is within the approved tolerance value need not be advised of the discrepancy. from question bank
explanation: No need to advise if within tolerance.

question_text: What shall be the action of a controller if the displayed Mode C level information is not within the approved tolerance value or when discrepancy in excess of the approved tolerance value is detected subsequent to verification: from question bank

options.A: Request the pilot to stop the Mode C transmission
options.B: Advised the pilot of the discrepancy.
options.C: Request the pilot to check pressure setting and confirm aircraft's level.
options.D: Both b) and c) are correct.

question_text: What shall be the action of the controller if, following confirmation of the correct pressure setting the discrepancy continues to exist? from question bank

options.B: Request the pilot to stop Mode C transmission if it does interrupt the operation of Mode A.
options.C: Inform the accepting unit of the action taken.
options.D: Both b) and c) are correct.

question_text: The criterion which shall be used to determine that a specific level is occupied by an aircraft shall be in RVSM airspace: from question bank

question_text: An aircraft is considered to be maintaining its assigned level in CVSM airspace as long as the SSR Mode C-derived level information indicates that it is within: from question bank

question_text: An aircraft cleared to leave a level is considered to have commenced its manoeuvre and vacated the previously occupied level when the pressure-altitude-derived level information indicates a change of in the anticipated direction from its previously assigned level: from question bank
explanation: Change of more than 300 ft indicates vacated.

question_text: An aircraft in climb or descent is considered to have crossed a level when the SSR Mode C-derived level information indicates that it has passed this level in the required direction by more than: from question bank
explanation: Crossed when passed by more than 300 ft.

question_text: An aircraft is considered to have reached the level to which it has been cleared when the elapsed time of three display updates, three sensor updates or , whichever is the greater, has passed since the pressure-altitude-derived level information has indicated that it is within the appropriate tolerances of the assigned level: from question bank

question_text: Which of the following action is required to be taken by the radar controller before taking over watch? from question bank

options.C: Get the actual/ forecast of upper winds.

explanation: All are required.

question_text: Before taking over the radar controller has to ensure that the technical officer is satisfied with the functional capabilities of the radar system as well as the information presented on the radar display. from question bank

explanation: Identification and informing pilot are required; clearance may already be given.

question_text: Till what time the identification of the aircraft which has been provided with radar service shall be maintained? from question bank

options.D: Observation of compliance with an instruction to squawk IDENT.
